analysis.mistake: Margin (mt-8) was applied to the outer header wrapper div instead of the .header-main navigation bar element, causing incorrect spacing above the nav container
analysis.expected: The margin-top should be on the .header-main element (the nav bar itself) to create space between the mobile CTA button and the nav bar
analysis.rootCause: Developer misinterpreted the layout structure. The outer wrapper is a flex container with gap-2 spacing between child elements. The gap already spaces the mobile CTA button from the nav bar. Applying mt-8 to the outer wrapper caused unwanted top margin on the entire header component, when the spacing intent was specific to the nav bar element. The specification or design reference likely showed the spacing visually as part of the nav bar's positioning, not the wrapper's margin.

suggestions.content: When applying margins/spacing to nested header structures with flex containers: if a flex parent uses `gap-*` to space children, do NOT add additional margins to the parent wrapper (e.g., mt-* on relative flex flex-col gap-2). Instead, apply spacing classes directly to the specific nested element that needs them (e.g., mt-8 on the nav bar itself). Gap handles inter-child spacing; margins on the wrapper create unintended outer spacing.
suggestions.rationale: Prevents confusion between flex gap spacing (controls space between siblings) and wrapper margins (controls space outside the flex container), a common source of layout mistakes in nested header structures.
